content.title: CHOOSING YOUR HERO
content.body:
Each player chooses one Hero to play. For this Adventure, the Party will use the following Heroes and Dungeon Roles:

  • Vorn ÔÇö Defender: protects allies and holds the frontline.
  • Lorelai ÔÇö Controller: moves enemies and creates openings.
  • Jaheen ÔÇö Support: heals, protects, and removes harmful effects.
  • Maya ÔÇö Striker: focuses on damage and priority targets.
  • Elros ÔÇö Leader: coordinates the Party and enhances his allies.

Choose the Hero that best matches how you want to play.

For this Start Here walkthrough, make sure one player chooses Vorn. The guided first turn uses Vorn as the example Hero, while the remaining players may choose any other Heroes.

Use the non-tutorial side of the Hero Board, as shown below:

content.title: VORN ÔÇô FRONTLINE DEFENDER
content.body:
Vorn protects the Party by standing between his allies and danger. He can gain Shield, prevent damage, and keep enemies focused on him.

For this Adventure, Vorn uses the Defender Dungeon Role.

Core Components

Take Vorn's:

  • Hero Board
  • Hero Card
  • Starting Equipment cards
  • Starting Action Cubes

Dungeon Role Skills

Take the 2 Defender Dungeon Role cards.

Progress Cards

Take all of VornÔÇÖs Hero Skill cards and Class Ability cards and keep them near his Hero Board. These cards are not available yet. You will unlock them as you progress during the Adventure.

content.title: LORELAI ÔÇô BATTLEFIELD CONTROLLER
content.body:
Lorelai controls the battlefield by moving enemies, disrupting their plans, and creating openings for the Party.

For this Adventure, Lorelai uses the Controller Dungeon Role.

Take LorelaiÔÇÖs:

  • Hero Board
  • Hero Card
  • Starting Equipment cards
  • Starting Action Cubes

Dungeon Role Skills

Take the 2 Controller Dungeon Role cards.

Progress Cards

Take all of LorelaiÔÇÖs Hero Skill cards and Class Ability cards and keep them near her Hero Board. These cards are not available yet. You will unlock them as you progress during the Adventure.

content.title: JAHEEN ÔÇô DEVOTED SUPPORT
content.body:
Jaheen keeps the Party standing by healing allies, protecting them from harm, and helping the group recover from dangerous effects.

For this Adventure, Jaheen uses the Support Dungeon Role.

Take JaheenÔÇÖs:

  • Hero Board
  • Hero Card
  • Starting Equipment cards
  • Starting Action Cubes

Dungeon Role Skills

Take the 2 Support Dungeon Role cards.

Progress Cards

Take all of JaheenÔÇÖs Hero Skill cards and Class Ability cards and keep them near his Hero Board. These cards are not available yet. You will unlock them as you progress during the Adventure.

content.title: MAYA ÔÇô DEADLY STRIKER
content.body:
Maya is the PartyÔÇÖs main damage dealer, focusing on precise attacks and taking down key enemies before they can threaten the group.

For this Adventure, Maya uses the Striker Dungeon Role.

Take MayaÔÇÖs:

  • Hero Board
  • Hero Card
  • Starting Equipment cards
  • Starting Action Cubes

Dungeon Role Skills

Take the 2 Striker Dungeon Role cards.

Progress Cards

Take all of MayaÔÇÖs Hero Skill cards and Class Ability cards and keep them near her Hero Board. These cards are not available yet. You will unlock them as you progress during the Adventure.

content.title: ELROS ÔÇö INSPIRING LEADER
content.body:
Elros leads the Party by coordinating his allies, allowing them to act beyond their turns and fight as a unified force.

For this Adventure, Elros uses the Leader Dungeon Role.

Take ElrosÔÇÖ:

  • Hero Board
  • Hero Card
  • Starting Equipment cards
  • Starting Action Cubes

Dungeon Role Skills

Take the 2 Leader Dungeon Role cards.

Progress Cards

Take all of ElrosÔÇÖ Hero Skill cards and Class Ability cards and keep them near his Hero Board. These cards are not available yet. You will unlock them as you progress during the Adventure.

content.title: PLAYER GIFTS
content.body:
For this Adventure, the Party receives a shared Gift Equipment Pack. Each Hero may choose 1 Gift Equipment card from this pack and equip it to the appropriate slot.

Remember to check your HeroÔÇÖs Proficiency at the bottom of the Hero Board before equipping a card.

These Gift Equipment cards are used during the Adventure and must be returned to the Store Owner at the end of the session.

For your first game, we recommend the following Gift Equipment for each Hero:

  • Vorn: Bulwark Shield ÔÇö This Shield gives you another way to Prevent damage for yourself or a teammate.
  • Lorelai: Bauble of Power ÔÇö This Trinket gives your magic Skills an incremental damage bonus.
  • Jaheen: Heroic Heart ÔÇö This Trinket improves your Cleanse effects, making you more effective as the PartyÔÇÖs Support.
  • Maya: Weakening Dagger ÔÇö This Dagger gives you some protection and another chance to trigger your Passive Skill, Best Friends.
  • Elros: Boots of Haste ÔÇô These Boots help you reach advantageous positions or make an extra attack to catch your foes by surprise.

Players who own or purchase a Chronicles of Drunagor Core box are also eligible to receive copies of these same Gift Equipment cards to keep, chosen from the cards still available at the store. Ask the Store Owner for details and availability.

Finishing the Setup

Place each Hero on the Initiative Track according to their Dungeon Role.

content.title: FIRST ROOM SETUP
content.body:
Next, prepare the First Room.

The image below shows the starting positions for all Heroes, Monsters, and Objects in the First Room.

Use the legend to place each component:

  • Each icon shows the exact space where that token or miniature must be placed.
  • The labels "2+," "3+," and "4+" indicate how many Heroes must be in the Party for that element to be used.

In Drunagor Nights, some setup instructions refer to White, Gray, or Black Monsters. These labels indicate the type of Monster to be used for that Adventure.

For this First Room, use the specific Monsters listed below so the Start Here examples are easier to follow:

  • White Monster: Dead Marksman
  • Gray Monster: Dire Headhunter

In later rooms, when the Adventure asks you to place White, Gray, or Black Monsters, you may either choose Monsters of the indicated type or randomize them from the available Monsters.

Set up the room according to the number of Heroes in the Party:

  • 2 Heroes: Place the Heroes, 2 Chest tokens, 2 Dire Headhunters, and 1 Dead Marksman.
  • 3 Heroes: Add 1 Dead Marksman in its marked position.
  • 4 Heroes: Add 1 Dire Headhunter and 1 Dead Marksman in their marked positions.

Monster Identification

For this First Room:

  • Use the first Monster Board to track the Dead Marksmen and Dire Headhunters.
  • Attach the Yellow, Dark Blue, and Pink snaps to the Dire Headhunters according to the number of Monsters in play.
  • Attach the Green, Red, and Black snaps to the Dead Marksmen according to the number of Monsters in play.

Then place all Monsters on their indicated spaces on the map.

Monsters of the same type may be placed in any order. You choose which snap color goes on each position.

Next, place a White Cube on the Monster Board to track each Monster's Health Points. Use the track that matches the color of that Monster's snap.

Each Monster's starting Health is shown on its Monster card. If a Monster gains a Condition during the Adventure, place the corresponding Condition token on the matching track of the Monster Board.

content.title: INITIATIVE TRACK
content.body:
Place the Monster cards on the Initiative Track according to the Rune shown on each card.

Normally, Monster cards, Minion cards, and Boss or Commander Attack cards are placed above or below the matching Rune on the Initiative Track. Heroes are placed above or below their Dungeon Roles.

content.title: YOUR FIRST TURN
content.body:
Now that everything is ready, it's time to begin the Adventure.

The following example demonstrates the basic flow of a round and introduces the core mechanics of play. After completing this walkthrough, you may continue the Adventure from that point or restart the round and play it again.

The Initiative Track shows that Vorn is the first Hero to act.

At the start of his turn, Vorn checks whether he is affected by any Conditions. He is not, so he proceeds to the Take Actions step.

For his first Cube Action, Vorn takes a Red Action Cube and places it on his Hammer Throw Skill.

Resolve the first effect of the Skill: Self: Shield 2. Vorn gains 2 Shield tokens.

Rule Tip ÔÇö Shield: Each Shield token prevents 1 damage. Stackable tokens, such as Shield, have a maximum of 4 tokens.

Next, resolve the damage effect. Hammer Throw deals 3 Damage to a target within range. Since Vorn used a Red Action Cube, the range is 1 Area.

The only enemy within range is the Red Dire Headhunter, so Vorn chooses it as the target and records 3 Damage on the Monster Board.

Now Vorn uses his Free Movement, repositioning himself next to the Dire Headhunter.

For his second Cube Action, Vorn uses a Yellow Action Cube and places it on his Guarding Blow Melee Skill.

First, resolve the SkillÔÇÖs Shield effect. Vorn may choose a target within Melee Range to gain Shield 2. He chooses one of his adjacent allies, who gains 2 Shield tokens.

Next, resolve the attack effect. Guarding Blow allows Vorn to make an attack roll with +5 to Hit.

Roll a D20 and add +5 to the result. If the final number is equal to or higher than VornÔÇÖs weapon Accuracy Value, the attack hits.

For this example, assume the attack is successful.

Vorn deals damage equal to his weapon Damage Attribute, which is 3 Damage, to the Dire Headhunter. Record this damage on the Monster Board.

After resolving all of his actions, Vorn ends his turn.

content.title: MONSTERSÔÇÖ TURN
content.body:
The next Character on the Initiative Track is the Dead Marksmen.

When activating Monsters, resolve them one at a time, following the color order shown on the Monster Board.

The first Monster to activate is the Green Dead Marksman.

Looking at the Dead Marksman card, we can see that its target priority is Strongest Hero. This means it will target the Hero with the highest current Health Points among those it can reach.

In this situation, that Hero is Vorn.

First, the Dead Marksman attempts to move into range of its target.

It needs to move 3 Squares to reach Range 1 of Vorn. Once in range, it attacks and deals 6 Damage.

Before the damage is applied, the Heroes have an opportunity to use Reactions.

To resolve the attack, first remove VornÔÇÖs 2 Shield tokens. Each Shield token prevents 1 Damage, so 4 Damage remain.

Each Hero may use 1 Reaction for each Hero being attacked.

Jaheen decides to react by using his Intervention Skill, which prevents 3 Damage.

After Shield and Prevention are applied, Vorn takes 1 Damage.

Since Vorn lost Health from the attack, he also suffers the MonsterÔÇÖs Collateral Damage and receives 2 Poison tokens. If all Damage had been prevented, Vorn would not suffer the Collateral Damage.

Rule Tip ÔÇö Poison: Poison deals damage to a Hero at the start of each of their turns. Vorn has 2 Poison tokens, so he will take 2 Damage at the start of each of his turns until the Poison is removed.

If Jaheen is not in the Party, Vorn can instead use his Parry Skill as a Reaction, preventing 3 Damage himself.

Tutorial Note: Vorn could also use his Parry Skill as a Reaction, in addition to JaheenÔÇÖs Intervention, to prevent the remaining Damage. This example does not use Parry so it can demonstrate how Collateral Damage and Poison work.

This concludes the activation of the first Dead Marksman.

Following the color order on the Monster Board, the next Monster to activate is the Red Dead Marksman. Its target is again the Strongest Hero, which is still Vorn.

It moves up to its full Movement value of 3 Squares toward Vorn, but this is not enough to reach Range 1. Since it cannot get within range, it ends its activation without attacking.

The same happens with the Black Dead Marksman: it moves as close as possible to Vorn, then ends its activation because it is still unable to attack.

This concludes the Dead Marksmen's activation.

The round now proceeds to the next Character on the Initiative Track.

content.title: OTHER IMPORTANT RULES
content.body:
Before you continue, review these short reminders. You do not need to memorize them now, but they may come up during the Adventure.

Rune Cards

Some Rune cards are already placed on the Initiative Track during setup. When the Initiative Marker reaches one of these Rune cards, pause and follow the instructions on that card.

Rune cards introduce Adventure mechanics that are active at that moment, such as events, hazards, objectives, or special effects.

Curse Cubes

Curse Cubes represent the physical, spiritual, and mental exhaustion suffered by the Heroes. Unlike Action Cubes, Curse Cubes are harmful.

Curse Cubes are Black cubes. When a Hero gains a Curse Cube, assign it to one of that HeroÔÇÖs Hero Skills or Dungeon Role Skills.

A Skill with a Curse Cube assigned to it is blocked and cannot be used until the Curse Cube is removed by a Cleanse effect.

If a Hero has 6 Curse Cubes on their board at the same time, that Hero is defeated and the Adventure immediately fails.

Recall Action

As Heroes use Skills, their Action Cube Reserve will eventually run low. A Recall Action lets you return all spent and Expended Action Cubes to your Reserve.

There are two types of Recall:

  • Willing Recall: At the end of your turn, if you have Action Cubes of 2 or fewer colors in your Reserve, you may Recall. If you do, your turn ends immediately.
  • Unwilling Recall: If you spend or Expend your last Action Cube and your Reserve becomes empty, you must Recall immediately. This can happen during or outside your turn, and it does not end the turn.

Whenever you perform a Recall, whether Willing or Unwilling, you suffer a Recall penalty: gain 1 Curse Cube and assign it to one of your Hero Skills or Dungeon Role Skills.

Knockout

When a HeroÔÇÖs Health is reduced to 0 or less, that Hero is Knocked Out.

Place their model lying down on the board, then:

  • Remove all Conditions from that Hero.
  • Recall all of their Action Cubes.
  • Assign 1 Trauma Cube to one of their Hero Skills or Dungeon Role Skills.

While Knocked Out, a Hero cannot act, use Skills, be targeted, take Damage, or receive Conditions.

At the beginning of that HeroÔÇÖs next turn, stand their model back up and restore their Health to the maximum. Beginning of Turn Skills work normally on that turn.

Chests

A Hero adjacent to a Chest may use a Minor Action to loot it.

When looting a Chest, draw 1 card from the Chest deck. As part of the same Action, the Hero may use the Item immediately or give it to another Hero at Any Range.

Chest Items are Consumables. They can be used once and are discarded after use.

Hero Progression

During the Adventure, your Hero will learn new Hero Skills and Class Abilities.

When your Hero learns a Level 1 Hero Skill, take the chosen Hero Skill card and 1 Action Cube of the matching color. Place both on your Hero Board.

When your Hero learns a Level 2 Hero Skill, flip that same Hero Skill card so both Skills are available. Then, take 1 additional Action Cube of the matching color and add it to your Hero Board.

A Hero cannot learn a Level 2 Hero Skill before learning the Level 1 Skill from the same card.

When your Hero learns a Class Ability, place the chosen Class Ability card next to your Hero Board. Then, take 1 White Cube and place it in the matching slot to mark the ability you learned.

A Hero cannot learn a Level 2 or Level 3 Class Ability before learning the previous ability from the same card.

Defeating Monsters

When a Monster's Health is reduced to 0 or less, it is defeated.

Remove its miniature from the map and remove its Health marker from the Monster Board. Any Conditions affecting that Monster are also removed.

Defeated Monsters no longer activate or affect the Adventure.

content.title: ADVENTURE RULES
content.body:
Before you continue, review the special rules that are active during this Adventure.

Defeat Conditions

The Adventure immediately fails if any of the following happens:

  • A Rune cannot be drawn from the bag.
  • Any Hero has 2 Trauma Cubes on their board at the same time.
  • Any Hero has 6 Curse Cubes on their board at the same time.

If any Hero is defeated, the Party cannot proceed.

Stirring Darkness

The Darkness is especially strong here.

There are only 12 Runes in the Rune bag. This is enough for 9 complete rounds before the Party risks failing to draw a Rune.

Plan your actions carefully and do not let the Adventure drag on for too long.

Continue the Adventure

This concludes this Start Here guide. Now, you can choose either to restart the Adventure or continue playing from the next characterÔÇÖs turn. If you have any questions regarding Keywords or the mechanics of the turn, feel free to check the Player Aid or the Keyword tab on the Drunagor App.
